mappings.rationale: Covers fundamental concepts of scarcity, utility, value, and market mechanisms for satisfying material wants.
Directly covers consumer behaviour, market structures, competition, monopoly, and price determination.
Covers factors influencing consumer tastes and spending, and departures from rational consumer behaviour (subsection B.2).
Covers the market economy and price system in capitalist economies (subsections C and D).
Covers strategic behaviour of sellers under competitive conditions including oligopoly (subsection C.1.b).
Covers advertising as a factor influencing consumer tastes and spending decisions (subsection B.2).
Covers marketing strategies that shape consumer behaviour and market functioning.
Covers branding as a factor influencing consumer preferences and market competition.
Directly covers government regulation of monopolistic practices and workable competition (subsections C.1.c and C.1.d).
Covers externalities like pollution where the price system fails, relevant to subsection D.2.c.
Covers debates about market deregulation, price controls, and the role of government in markets.
Covers international markets and global trade as extensions of market mechanisms.
Covers foundational market theory including competition, pricing, and the relationship of markets to social welfare (subsection C.4).
Covers the counterpart of the market under economic planning and markets under socialism (subsection C.3).
